name: Jaduguda
notes: India's first uranium mine (1967-). Ore in autoclastic conglomerate & quartz-chlorite-apatite-tourmaline-magnetite schist. Dip 60-70 deg N (west part), 10-20 deg N (central/east).

name: Bhatin
notes: Small underground deposit ~3km from Jaduguda, shares infrastructure. Horizontal cut-and-fill mining.

name: Narwapahar
notes: 12km west of Jaduguda, commissioned 1995. Cut-and-fill stoping.

name: Banduhurang
notes: Open-pit mine. Mineralisation in chlorite & feldspathic-chlorite schist, E-W striking ore body, variable dip 10-70 deg N. [Corrected 2026-07: center shifted 1084 m south to match mindat.org + Global Energy Observatory coordinates and the EIA TOR toposheet bound -- the original center sat outside the toposheet's own northern edge.]

name: Mohuldih
notes: 5km west of Turamdih. Mineralisation established over 1km strike length, ~250m vertical depth. Mining since April 2012. [Corrected 2026-07: district was mis-tagged East Singhbhum; verified as Saraikela Kharsawan (Gamharia/Rajnagar block) via independent sources and point-in-polygon test against District_Boundary_JH.geojson.]

name: Bagjata
notes: Southern extension of Singhbhum belt near Phuljhari. Underground inclined mine.

name: Singhbhum Thrust Belt (regional envelope)
notes: Approximate envelope enclosing the known deposit cluster along the Singhbhum Shear Zone. NOT a surveyed boundary -- illustrative only, for showing the overall trend that hosts all deposits above. Also includes minor/sub-economic occurrences: Kanyaluka, Nimdih, Nandup, Keruadungri (AMD-documented small sub-economic occurrences along the belt). NOT included here: the Khadandungri-Sungri block (East Singhbhum), for which the Centre approved Stage-1 uranium prospecting in 2025 -- no public coordinates exist yet (news coverage gives only the block name and a 0.225 ha prospecting area); re-check when AMD/UCIL publish exact boundaries rather than fabricate one.
